checks:
Caption-VO mismatch — compare every caption line against the transcript word by word, from first to last. Flag lines where a word is wrong, missing, added, or in the wrong tense.

CRITICAL RULE BEFORE FLAGGING ANY MISMATCH: If the differing word is an ingredient name, botanical name, brand name, product name, or medical/technical term — STOP. Do NOT flag it. Whisper (the transcription model) consistently mishears specialized vocabulary. The caption is correct; the transcript is wrong. Examples of what NOT to flag: transcript says 'Bakuccio' but caption says 'Bakuchiol' — do not flag. Transcript says 'Ajatica' but caption says 'Asiatica' — do not flag. Transcript says 'power camo' but caption says 'Power Camouflage Redness Defense' — do not flag. Only flag a mismatch when the differing word is a plain everyday English word where the caption clearly reads differently from what was said.

WHEN FLAGGING A MISMATCH: Always include a 'Probable correct word' in your description. State which word appears in the caption, which word appears in the transcript, and then give your best judgment on which is likely correct and why. Example format: 'Caption says "helps" — transcript says "helped." Probable correct word: "helps" (caption likely accurate; Whisper may have added a letter). Reviewer to confirm.' This applies even when you are uncertain — always give a probable answer so the reviewer can quickly verify rather than re-watch from scratch.

SEVERITY RULE FOR MISMATCHES: If your probable correct word is the CAPTION (meaning Whisper is wrong), set severity to Minor — the caption is already correct and the reviewer just needs to confirm. Only set Major or Critical if your probable correct word is the TRANSCRIPT (meaning the caption itself is likely wrong and needs to be fixed).
Duplicate captions — SKIP THIS CHECK ENTIRELY. Do not flag duplicate captions under any circumstances. This check has been removed from the captions agent.
Sentence starts without a capital letter — only when you are certain it is the START of a new sentence, not a continuation of the previous line.

Wrong sentence splitting — flag when captions do not follow natural sentence boundaries as spoken by the talent. Use the transcript to identify where sentences end (periods, exclamation marks, question marks). Flag when: (1) two complete sentences are merged into one caption line — e.g. 'My monthly ThreadBeast box just arrived you take a quick style quiz' should be two separate captions; (2) a caption breaks mid-phrase or mid-clause in an unnatural place — e.g. splitting 'you take a quick' / 'style quiz' instead of keeping the phrase together; (3) a sentence continues across multiple captions when it should be one. If a script or context is provided above, use it as the reference for where sentences should start and end. Flag each instance with timestamp and show what the current split is vs what it should be.

Caption split typo — DO NOT search for split typos by scanning frames yourself. The system has already run a programmatic detector that identified all confirmed split errors. Those errors are listed explicitly in the CAPTION SPLIT ERRORS DETECTED section of the audio context above. Your job is only to flag the ones listed there — report each one exactly as provided. If the CAPTION SPLIT ERRORS section is empty or absent, there are no split typos to flag. Do not add any split typos of your own.
Captions out of sync — footage clearly changes scenes or actions while the caption still shows the previous line, or captions appear before the matching words are spoken.
Typo in caption text — a word on screen is misspelled. Cross-reference against transcript to confirm the correct word. Flag the exact wrong word, timestamp, and what it should say.
Captions outside safe area — caption text is cut off at frame edges or positioned so close to the bottom or sides that it would be cropped on standard displays.

Missing disclaimer — if the video makes health claims, financial claims, results claims, or promotes a regulated product or service, check whether a disclaimer or legal text appears on screen at any point. Flag only when the content clearly warrants a disclaimer and none is present.

Incorrect pronunciation — review the transcript for words the talent appears to have mispronounced. A mispronunciation often shows in the transcript as an unexpected phonetic substitute. Only flag when you can confirm from context (brand name, medical term, product name) that the transcribed word differs from the intended word. Flag timestamp, word as heard, and what it should be. Do NOT flag if the difference could be a Whisper misread of a correctly pronounced specialized term.
do_not_flag: Lowercase mid-sentence continuations — only flag capitals at the start of new sentences

Specialized vocabulary mismatches driven by Whisper alone — trust the caption for ingredient, brand, botanical, and medical names unless the caption itself is visually wrong. This includes ALL brand names no matter how distorted Whisper renders them — examples: Whisper says 'Sauta' but caption says 'Zara' — do not flag. Whisper says 'Threadmease' but caption says 'Threadbeast' — do not flag. Whisper says 'Fuddies' but caption says 'Hoodies' — do not flag. If the caption word is a recognizable brand, product, or clothing term and the transcript word is clearly a phonetic distortion, skip it entirely.
Caption split typos found by your own visual scanning — the programmatic detector handles this. Only flag what is listed in the CAPTION SPLIT ERRORS DETECTED context
Single caption wrapping to two lines — that is normal, not a duplicate
Duplicate captions of any kind — this check is disabled. Never flag duplicate captions

Single-syllable preposition or article substitutions — words like 'an/in', 'on/in', 'at/to', 'a/the', 'of/for' where the transcript and caption differ by one letter. Whisper routinely mishears these. Do not flag unless the meaning is dramatically changed

Caption-VO mismatches where the transcript word sounds phonetically similar to what is on screen but doesn't make sense — if the caption reads correctly in context and the transcript sounds like a mishear of the same phrase, trust the caption and do not flag
Audio clipping, audio levels too hot, distortion, loudness, crackling — these are 100% the visual agent's job. Never flag any audio level or quality issue as a captions agent

Dead air, silence gaps, audio pauses, silence at end of video — these are 100% the visual agent's job. Never flag silence or dead air under Captions under any circumstances, even if there are no captions on screen during the silence. Do not create a captions issue for silence, ever.
VO pronunciation errors that are the same word and same timestamp as a caption-VO mismatch you are already flagging — do not create a duplicate entry for the same moment
Any issue you cannot visually confirm from the frames or transcript — do not flag based on assumption
